result:
All fixes are implemented and verified with a real production build. Summary:

**Diagnosed** (via live-site inspection + GSC data + source review): the SaaS pivoted from InboxZap (email cleanup) to Lumina (AI meeting assistant), and the repo hadn't caught up — stale brand metadata, two live routes (`/features`, `/pricing`) missing from source entirely, a blog fetching from a dead API endpoint, and 12 already-indexed blog posts (~12,880 GSC impressions) completely orphaned with no links pointing to them anywhere on the site.

**Fixed in the repo** (build passes, verified against a locally-run production server):
- `app/layout.tsx` — replaced stale "InboxZap" default metadata with Lumina branding, added `metadataBase`, added a footer rebrand note ("Formerly InboxZap…") to capture the site's best-performing query (brand searches, pos. 4.5, 3% CTR)
- `app/page.tsx`, `app/features/page.tsx` (new), `app/pricing/page.tsx` (new) — full per-page metadata (title/description/canonical/OG/Twitter), homepage JSON-LD (Organization + SoftwareApplication), fixed the duplicate `<h1>` on Features
- `app/lib/posts.ts` (new) + `app/blog/page.tsx` + `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` (new) — blog is now server-rendered from real data instead of a broken client fetch; all 12 legacy posts are linked, statically generated, and carry meta descriptions + `BlogPosting` JSON-LD; two posts got a natural one-line bridge to Lumina where the topic overlaps
- `app/sitemap.ts` — now includes all 12 post URLs alongside the static pages
- `public/img/og-lumina.png` (new) — fixes the 404'ing OG image referenced site-wide

**Deliverables:** `FULL-AUDIT-REPORT.md` and `ACTION-PLAN.md` in the workspace root, with evidence, severity/confidence labels, and remaining open items (real OG image design, CWV instrumentation — not measurable in this sandbox, no network access for PageSpeed API).
